patterns_discovered.description: Service uses Redis as primary storage with cache utilities (getCache, setCache, deleteCache) for session-based cart persistence
examples: saveCart() persists to Redis with TTL
Cart TTL set to 24 hours (86400 seconds)

patterns_discovered.description: All operations use organizationId for multi-tenant isolation, cart keys prefixed with org context
Service emits custom events for cart state changes enabling downstream processing
All user inputs validated against schemas before processing
Cart items store snapshots of product data (name, variant name) to preserve historical accuracy
examples: productName and variantName stored with item
Decouples item data from product catalog changes

patterns_discovered.description: Identical items (same product, variant, modifiers) automatically merge with quantity increment
Item updates trigger recalculation of cart totals including tax and discounts
examples: recalculateCartTotals() updates subtotal, tax, discount, total

gotchas_discovered.description: Service uses generateItemId() method but implementation appears to rely on external generation logic
gotchas_discovered.impact: Medium - Could fail if ID generation not properly implemented
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Should verify generateItemId() uses UUID or sequential generation

gotchas_discovered.description: Service accepts ProductInfo parameter but caller must fetch from product service - no internal resolution
gotchas_discovered.impact: Medium - Tight coupling to caller; no circuit breaker pattern
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Callers must handle product service failures; consider abstracting to injectable ProductRepository

gotchas_discovered.description: Each modifier adds price to item total independently; no validation of modifier quantity logic
gotchas_discovered.impact: Low - Works as designed but could lead to unexpected pricing if modifiers have complex rules
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider adding modifier validation schema for complex scenarios

gotchas_discovered.description: DEFAULT_TAX_RATE (10%) is hard-coded; no per-organization or per-product tax rate configuration
gotchas_discovered.impact: High - Limits business flexibility for multi-region deployments
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Should move tax rate to organization settings or configuration service

gotchas_discovered.description: No transaction-like guarantees between cart modifications and Redis save operations
gotchas_discovered.impact: Medium - In high-concurrency scenarios, concurrent modifications could lose data
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider implementing Redis transactions or optimistic locking with version numbers

gotchas_discovered.description: MAX_CART_ITEMS set to 50 items; no bulk operation support or batching
gotchas_discovered.impact: Low - May frustrate users in high-volume scenarios but protects service stability
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document limitation; consider progressive loading for cart display

gotchas_discovered.description: modifiersMatch() function implementation not visible in provided diff; deep equality comparison could be fragile
gotchas_discovered.impact: Medium - Risk of item duplication if comparison fails
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify modifiersMatch() uses stable sorting and complete comparison

approach_outcome.description: Created comprehensive Cart service with full CRUD operations, caching, event emission, and validation
approach_outcome.completeness: Complete - All core cart functionality implemented
approach_outcome.code_quality: High - Well-documented with JSDoc, clear separation of concerns, comprehensive type safety
notable_features: Automatic item merging for identical products
Product data snapshots for historical accuracy

recommendations.recommendation: Externalize hard-coded values (TAX_RATE, CART_TTL_SECONDS, MAX_CART_ITEMS) to environment configuration or organization settings service
recommendations.rationale: Enables multi-region support and per-tenant customization without code changes

recommendations.recommendation: Implement optimistic locking or Redis transactions for cart modifications to prevent lost updates in concurrent scenarios
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation lacks transaction guarantees; could lose data under race conditions

recommendations.recommendation: Create injectable ProductRepository interface to abstract product data fetching, enabling caching and fallback strategies
recommendations.rationale: Currently tight coupling to caller for product resolution; limits reusability and error handling

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive unit tests covering: item merging logic, tax calculations, modifier resolution, concurrent modifications, and edge cases
recommendations.rationale: 1021-line service with complex logic requires thorough test coverage; none visible in session data

recommendations.recommendation: Add rate limiting for add/update operations and implement graceful degradation for Redis failures
recommendations.rationale: Prevents abuse; ensures service resilience if Redis becomes unavailable

recommendations.recommendation: Consider implementing cart compression or archival for carts exceeding 24-hour TTL to analyze abandonment patterns
recommendations.rationale: Could provide business intelligence on user behavior without impacting live cart performance

recommendations.recommendation: Add integration guide documenting how ProductService integrations should work and expected ProductInfo structure
recommendations.rationale: Clarifies contract between CartService and calling code; reduces integration errors
